text: The video shows a laparoscopic technique for mesh removal using a TAPP approach. The surgeon carefully dissects the peritoneum, revealing a distorted anatomy with an obliterated reoperative plane. An old mesh plug is visible, adherent to the spermatic cord, making it challenging to distinguish the interface between the mesh and the cord.

The robotic technique is demonstrated for the removal of a clam-shelled TEP mesh causing chronic pain. The surgeon navigates the distorted anatomy, carefully avoiding the anterior epigastric vessels and posterior iliac vessels, while being mindful of the proximity to the bladder and bowel.
